mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) following rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 10 percent, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 10 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous surge in household savings and a contraction in GDP indicates forced saving during an economic shutdown, which fuels a delayed equity rebound as liquidity seeks yield over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in 70 percent of historical forced-saving episodes, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During acute supply chain disruptions, a month-over-month rebound in industrial production signals capacity restoration and margin expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in 60 percent of months following an INDPRO rebound during supply disruptions, or the mechanism shows zero predictive accuracy (Rank IC = 0) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which consistently generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields negative forward 20-day returns in 55 percent of observations, or the Rank IC drops below 0.015 across all 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation accelerates above 3 percent annually, central banks initiate aggressive rate hikes that compress equity multiples and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3 percent threshold, or the negative return signal fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales indicates a shift in consumer capital allocation toward real assets, which supports equity valuations through construction sector earnings over the next month.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence predicts negative 20-day returns in 65 percent of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes (p > 0.10) when controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound monetary environment, a rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals effective fiscal stimulus transmission, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as liquidity chases risk assets.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in 70 percent of months where UNRATE falls while FEDFUNDS remains at zero, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward testing.
